full_response:
Let

S  : King Arthur’s “ground” frame (in which the racetrack is at rest)  
S₂ : the inertial frame that is comoving with Knight #2 during the running
      part of the race.  The two frames are chosen so that their spatial
      axes are parallel and their origins coincide at the instant Knight #2
      leaves the starting line (event O).

Given in frame S (data supplied earlier in the problem)

• Length of the track (start → finish): L  
• Speed of Knight #1: v₁ (β₁ = v₁ /c)  
• Speed of Knight #2: v₂ (β₂ = v₂ /c), with v₂  >  v₁

Because King Arthur arranges the start‐delay so that the two knights
reach the finish simultaneously in S,

T   = time of arrival (event E_F) in S = L / v₁                   (1)

Δt  = delay before Knight #2 starts  =  T – L / v₂                (2)

Hence the coordinates of the two key events in S are

O (start of #2) : (t = Δt, x = 0)  
E_F (finish)  : (t = T,   x = L)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------
Lorentz transformation from S to S₂
--------------------------------------------------------------------------

S₂ moves with velocity +v₂ e_x with respect to S, so, using
the standard Lorentz matrix (origins coincident at O):

Δt₂ = γ₂ ( Δt_S – β₂ Δx_S )                    (3a)  
Δx₂ = γ₂ ( Δx_S – β₂ Δt_S )                    (3b)

where

Δt_S = T – Δt = L / v₂                         (4)  
Δx_S = L                                        (5)  
γ₂   ≡ 1 /√(1 – β₂²).                           (6)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------
Spatial coordinate of the finish in S₂
--------------------------------------------------------------------------

Δx₂ = γ₂ ( L – v₂ · L / v₂ ) = γ₂ ( L – L ) = 0.                  (7)

As expected, the finish line meets Knight #2 at his own spatial origin.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------
Temporal coordinate of the finish in S₂
--------------------------------------------------------------------------

Δt₂ = γ₂ ( L / v₂ – v₂ L / c² )
     = γ₂ L ( 1/v₂ – v₂ / c² )
     = γ₂ L (1 – β₂²) / v₂
     = ( L / v₂ ) √(1 – β₂² ).                                     (8)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------
Result
--------------------------------------------------------------------------

Therefore, in Knight #2’s frame the event “both knights reach the
finish line” has coordinates

t₂ =  (L/c) √(1 – β₂²) / β₂,          x₂ = 0.       

For the numerical values used in the problem set (β₂ = 0.8,
γ₂ = 5/3),

t₂ = 0.75 L / c, x₂ = 0.                               (9)

\boxed{(t_{2},\,x_{2})=\Bigl(\dfrac{L}{c}\,\dfrac{\sqrt{1-\beta_{2}^{2}}}{\beta_{2}},\;0\Bigr)}  

Confidence: 29%
